title: Java Objects
content:
### Objects in Java

An object is an instance of a class. It has state (fields) and behavior (methods).

---

### Creating Objects

Objects are created using the `new` keyword:

```java
ClassName objectName = new ClassName();
```

---

### Object Lifecycle

| Stage | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Declaration | `Car myCar;` (reference variable) |
| Instantiation | `new Car()` (memory allocation) |
| Initialization | Constructor executes |
| Usage | Access fields and methods |
| Garbage Collection | JVM reclaims memory |

---

### Accessing Object Members

Use the dot operator (`.`):

```java
myCar.brand = "Toyota";   // Access field
myCar.start();             // Call method
```

---

### Multiple Objects

Each object has its own copy of instance fields:

```java
Car car1 = new Car("Toyota", 2020);
Car car2 = new Car("Honda", 2022);
// car1 and car2 are independent
```

> **Key Point:** The `new` keyword allocates memory on the heap. Multiple objects from the same class are independent. Objects are reference types — variables hold references to the memory location, not the object itself.

quiz.question: Where are objects stored in memory?
